summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a late‑1989 Dead show, highlighted by a soaring Foolish Heart and a fired‑up The Other One.
review: The recording is praised as far superior to other SBD sources – crisp, clear and “light years better” than comparable tapes.  Musically the concert is described as a strong, energetic performance with standout moments such as Foolish Heart (despite one mixed comment on Garcia’s vocal), The Other One, Crazy Fingers > Stella Blue and a solid Playing in the Band jam.  Overall the consensus is highly positive, with only minor criticism of a few vocal passages.

ai_rating.rationale: All ten reviews are favorable; recording quality is repeatedly called excellent and the show is described as fantastic with only one modest complaint about vocals.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers note this SBD is clearer, crisper and “light years better” than other soundboard copies; no drop‑outs or noise issues reported.

poor_songs: Foolish Heart (vocals inaudible on opening verses)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is praised for its high energy and solid pacing; reviewers wish Jack Straw had appeared later, but overall the flow from first to second set feels strong and satisfying.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s playing is lauded (best Foolish Heart) though one reviewer notes his opening verses on Foolish Heart are barely audible, reflecting a common early‑90s vocal issue.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drumming receives positive remarks, described as great and driving the jams.
